name: Polynomial Roots and Vieta's Formulas
description: Relating coefficients of a polynomial to sums and products of its roots, including cases with non-real roots.

name: Solving Systems of Equations
description: Techniques for solving linear and non-linear systems of equations, including those with integer or real constraints.

name: Logarithmic Properties and Equations
description: Applying properties of logarithms (e.g., change of base, product/quotient rules) to solve equations.

name: Binomial Theorem and Expansions
description: Using the binomial theorem for series expansion and counting terms.

name: Greatest Integer Function (Floor/Ceiling)
description: Applying properties of floor and ceiling functions in equations and inequalities.

name: Complex Number Operations and Properties
description: Working with complex numbers, including roots of unity, modulus, argument, and polynomial roots.

name: Arithmetic and Geometric Sequences/Series
description: Properties, sums, and recursive definitions of arithmetic and geometric progressions.

name: Radical Expressions Simplification
description: Techniques for simplifying expressions involving square roots and other radicals.

name: Fraction Operations and Properties
description: Basic operations and properties of fractions, including reducing to lowest terms.

name: Functional Equations
description: Solving equations that define properties of functions.

name: Kinematics (Motion Problems)
description: Solving problems involving distance, speed, time, and relative motion.

name: Euclidean Geometry of Shapes and Properties
description: Fundamental properties of triangles (area, perimeter, angles, bisectors, medians) and quadrilaterals (rectangles, rhombuses, trapezoids), including congruence and similarity, and regular polygons.

name: Circle Properties (Tangents, Chords, In/Circumcircles)
description: Theorems and properties related to circles, including tangents, chords, inscribed/circumscribed circles, and power of a point.

name: Coordinate Geometry and Transformations
description: Solving geometric problems using coordinates, including distances, areas, lines, reflections, and rotations.

name: Volume and Surface Area of Solids
description: Calculating volumes and surface areas of polyhedra (e.g., pyramids, prisms, cubes) and cones, including Euler's formula for polyhedra.

name: Geometric Similarity
description: Using properties of similar figures (e.g., triangles, prisms) to find unknown lengths, areas, or volumes.

name: Divisibility and Number of Divisors
description: Counting positive divisors, properties of divisibility, GCD, and LCM.

name: Modular Arithmetic and Congruences
description: Working with remainders, congruences, and properties of numbers in different bases.

name: Prime Numbers and Factorization
description: Properties of prime numbers and prime factorization, including factorial tails (number of trailing zeros).

name: Properties of Integers (Perfect Squares/Cubes)
description: Identifying and using properties of perfect squares, cubes, and sums of consecutive integers.

name: Number Base Representation
description: Converting and working with numbers in different bases (e.g., base 10, base 8, base 11).

name: Diophantine Equations
description: Solving equations where only integer solutions are sought.

name: Rational Number Properties
description: Properties of rational numbers, including representation in lowest terms.

name: Fundamental Counting Principles
description: Applying permutation, combination, and inclusion-exclusion principles to count arrangements, selections, and specific configurations.

name: Probability Theory (Basic, Conditional, Geometric)
description: Calculating probabilities for various events, including conditional probability, geometric probability, and expected values.

name: Game Theory (Strategies)
description: Analyzing winning strategies in simple combinatorial games.

name: Trigonometric Identities and Equations
description: Applying trigonometric identities and solving trigonometric equations, often in geometric contexts.
